content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 14, 1989

Mark Neveau, President
Fremont Fire Fighters
2830 Barrington Terrace
Fremont, CA 94536

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File Number A-89-064

Dear Mr. Neveau:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act of 1974.1

QUESTION

May the Fremont Fire Fighters Political Action Committee
reimburse you for contributions you made to candidates on behalf
of the PAC during the November 1988 election.

ANSWER

The PAC may reimburse you for the contributions you made on
its behalf during the November 1988 election.

FACTS

You personally incurred $479.51 of monetary and non-monetary
contributions on behalf of the Fremont Fire Fighters Political
Action Committee to support five candidates at various offices
throughout the Bay Area in the November 1988 election. The
Fremont Fire Fighters Political Action Committee bank account did
not have sufficient funds at that time to make the expenditures,
and, due to a lack of a required quorum at membership meetings to
transfer funds from one account to another, the committee was
unable to transfer the necessary funds into the PAC account to
cover the expenses.

ANALYSIS

Section 84302 prohibits anyone from making a contribution on
behalf of another unless the person making the contribution (the
intermediary) discloses to the recipient his or her own name,
address, occupation and employer and the name, address, occupation
and employer of the actual source of the contribution.

___________
1Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Neveau, Mark
Page Two

     To report actions taken by you on behalf of the PAC, the PAC
must report the contributions you made to the various candidates
mentioned in your letter as in-kind loans to the PAC. On its next
report for the period in which you made the contributions, the PAC
must report the payments you made as in-kind loans on Schedule C
("non-monetary contributions") of Form 420. Additionally, the
payments must be reported on Schedule F of Form 420 as accrued
expenses owed to you. In addition, the PAC must disclose the
name, address and amount paid to any vendor(s) to whom you made
payments of $100 or more, and must disclose the name of each
candidate or committee to whom you made a contribution of $100 or
more. The PAC also must disclose the contributions on the
Allocation Page at the front of Form 420. When the PAC repays
you, the repayments are shown as payments of accrued expenses on
Line 4 of Schedule E ("Expenditures") and Line 4 of Schedule F of
the Form 420.

     You should note that effective January 1, 1989, candidates
may no longer transfer or loan campaign funds to other candidates.
(Section 85304.)

     If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                   By:  Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
                                        Political Reform Consultant

FREMONT FIRE FIGHTERS
INTERNATIONAL ASSOCIATION OF FIRE FIGHTERS - LOCAL 1689

AFFILIATED WITH

AFL CIO
CENTRAL LABOR COUNCIL
OF ALAMEDA COUNTY
INTERNATIONAL ASSOCIATION
OF FIRE FIGHTERS
CALIFORNIA LABOR COPE

P O BOX 1134

FREMONT, CALIFORNIA - 94538
415 651-1115

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J ST
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814

JANUARY 26, 1989

DEAR MS. TAYLOR:

AS PER OUR CONVERSATION ON THIS DATE I AM FORMALLY REQUESTING A WRITTEN CON-
FIRMATION OF THE F.P.F.C. DECISION REGARDING A REIMBURSEMENT OF FUNDS FOR
EXPENSES INCURRED DURING THE NOVEMBER 1988 ELECTION. THE DETAILS OF THE
SITUATION IS AS FOLLOWS:

1. I PERSONALLY INCURRED $479.51 WORTH OF POLITICAL EXPENSES DURING THE
NOVEMBER 1988 ELECTION. THE EXPENSES INCURRED RELATED TO A $250.00
CONTRIBUTION TO A CANDIDATE FOR THE BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT BOARD OF
DIRECTORS ELECTION AND $229.51 WORTH OF IN-KINDS TO TWO CANDIDATES
IN THE WASHINGTON HOSPITAL BOARD OF DIRECTORS ELECTION AND TWO CAND-
IDATES IN THE FREMONT UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ELECTION.

2. OUR POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE ACCOUNT DID NOT HAVE ENOUGH MONEY TO
PAY THE EXPENSES. WE ALSO SUFFERED THE INABILITY TO TRANSFER MONEY
INTO THE PAC ACCOUNT DUE TO THE LACK OF A QUORUM AT OUR MEMBERSHIP
MEETINGS OF SEPTEMBER AND OCTOBER. (TO TRANSFER INTEREST EARNED MONEY
FROM OUR "DUES" ACCOUNT WE REQUIRE A VOTE OF THE MEMBERSHIP).

3. DUE TO THE ABOVE STATED REASON I PAID THE EXPENSES OUT OF MY PERSONAL
ACCOUNT ACTING AS AN INTERMEDIARY AND/OR REPRESENTATIVE OF THE FREMONT
FIRE FIGHTERS' UNION.

4. ON JANUARY 25, 1989 OUR MEMBERSHIP VOTED TO TRANSFER THE NECESSARY FUNDS
FOR REIMBURSEMENT INTO THE P.A.C. ACCOUNT.

THEREFORE I HAVE REQUESTED AN OPINION FROM YOUR ORGANIZATION REGARDING THE SIT-
UATION. IN OUR CONVERSATION YOU INFORMED ME THAT THE REIMBURSMENT ($479.51)
WOULD BE LEGAL AND YOU INSTRUCTED US ON HOW TO RECORD THIS TRANSACTION ON OUR
NEXT FILING.

I AM RESPECTFULLY REQUESTING A WRITTEN CONFIRMATION OF YOUR ORGANIZATIONS'
DECISION. PLEASE SEND THE CORRESPONDENCE TO:

MARK NEVEAU, PRESIDENT
2830 BARRINGTON TR.
FREMONT, CALIFORNIA 94536

IF YOU HAVE ANY QUESTIONS PLEASE DO NOT HESITATE TO CALL ME AT (415) 792-5508.

CORDIALLY

Mark Neveau

MARK NEVEAU: PRESIDENT

89-064

FREMONT FIRE FIGHTERS
INTERNATIONAL ASSOCIATION OF FIRE FIGHTERS - LOCAL 1689
FPPC
P.O. BOX 1134
AFFILIATED WITH
FREMONT, CALIFORNIA • 94538
AFL-CIO
415/651-1115
CENTRAL LABOR COUNCIL
OF ALAMEDA COUNTY
INTERNATIONAL ASSOCIATION
OF FIRE FIGHTERS
CALIFORNIA LABOR COPE

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION                    JANUARY 26, 1989
428 J ST
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA  95814

DEAR MS. TAYLOR:

AS PER OUR CONVERSATION ON THIS DATE I AM FORMALLY REQUESTING A WRITTEN CON-
FIRMATION OF THE F.P.P.C. DECISION REGARDING A REIMBURSEMENT OF FUNDS FOR
EXPENSES INCURRED DURING NOVEMBER 1988 ELECTION. THE DETAIL OF THE
SITUATION IS AS FOLLOWS:

1.  I PERSONALLY INCURRED $479.51 WORTH OF POLITICAL EXPENSES DURING THE
    NOVEMBER 1988 ELECTION. THE EXPENSES INCURRED RELATED TO A $250.00
    CONTRIBUTION TO A CANDIDATE FOR THE BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT BOARD OF
    DIRECTORS ELECTION AND $229.51 WORTH OF IN-KINDS TO TWO CANDIDATES
    IN THE WASHINGTON HOSPITAL BOARD OF DIRECTORS ELECTION AND TWO CAND-
    IDATES IN THE FREMONT UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ELECTION.

2.  OUR POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE ACCOUNT DID NOT HAVE ENOUGH MONEY TO
    PAY THE EXPENSES. WE ALSO SUFFERED THE INABILITY TO TRANSFER MONEY
    INTO THE PAC ACCOUNT DUE TO THE LACK OF A QUORUM AT OUR MEMBERSHIP
    MEETINGS OF SEPTEMBER AND OCTOBER. (TO TRANSFER INTEREST EARNED MONEY
    FROM OUR "DUES" ACCOUNT WE REQUIRE A VOTE OF THE MEMBERSHIP).

3.  DUE TO THE ABOVE STATED REASON I PAID THE EXPENSES OUT OF MY PERSONAL
    ACCOUNT ACTING AS AN INTERMEDIARY AND/OR REPRESENTATIVE OF THE FREMONT
    FIRE FIGHTERS' UNION.

4.  ON JANUARY 25, 1989 OUR MEMBERSHIP VOTED TO TRANSFER THE NECESSARY FUNDS
    FOR REIMBURSEMENT INTO THE F.A.C. ACCOUNT.

THEREFORE I HAVE REQUESTED AN OPINION FROM YOUR ORGANIZATION REGARDING THE SIT-
UATION. IN OUR CONVERSATION YOU INFORMED ME THAT THE REIMBURSEMENT ($479.51)
WOULD BE LEGAL AND YOU INSTRUCTED US ON HOW TO RECORD THIS TRANSACTION ON OUR
NEXT FILING.

I AM RESPECTFULLY REQUESTING A WRITTEN CONFIRMATION OF YOUR ORGANIZATIONS'
DECISION. PLEASE SEND THE CORRESPONDENCE TO:

                    MARK NEVEAU, PRESIDENT
                    2830 BARRINGTON TR.
                    FREMONT, CALIFORNIA 94536

IF YOU HAVE ANY QUESTIONS PLEASE DO NOT HESITATE TO CALL ME AT (415) 792-5508.

                         CORDIALLY,

                         [signature]
                         MARK NEVEAU; PRESIDENT

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 1, 1989


Mark Neveau
Fremont Fire Fighters
P.O. Box 1134
Fremont, CA  94538

Re:  Letter No. 89-064

Dear Mr. Neveau:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on January 30, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days of your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division
